message:
fix(fr-runtime): CONFIG SET maxmemory accepts memtoull suffix forms [bd-frankenredis-ky4n]

CONFIG SET maxmemory rejected "100mb" / "1gb" / "500k" because the
parser used parse_i64_arg which only handles plain integers.
Upstream Redis 7.2 (config.c::numericParseString → util.c::memtoull)
accepts:
  - 100kb / 100mb / 100gb (1024-base)
  - 100k / 100m / 100g (1000-base)
  - 100b or 100 (raw bytes)

Adds parse_memory_size_arg helper mirroring memtoull semantics
(case-insensitive suffix, rejects negative values and unknown
suffixes, overflow-safe via checked_mul). Wires it into the
CONFIG SET maxmemory branch.

Verified byte-for-byte against vendored Redis 7.2.4: 100mb /
1gb / 500k / 100kb / 100b all return OK and CONFIG GET maxmemory
returns the canonical decimal byte count (e.g. 100mb → 104857600).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
